methods.description: This method involves dating charcoal fragments using radiocarbon dating and calibrating the dates using the SHCal13 calibration curve to generate cumulative probabilities of fire events.

steps.description: Charcoal fragments >1 mm were submitted to DirectAMS for radiocarbon dating.
steps.searchable_summary: submit charcoal fragments to DirectAMS for radiocarbon dating

steps.description: The 'Bchron' package for R was used to calibrate the radiocarbon dates using the SHCal13 calibration curve.
steps.searchable_summary: calibrate radiocarbon dates using Bchron package and SHCal13 curve

steps.description: Generate cumulative probabilities of fire events.
steps.searchable_summary: generate cumulative probabilities of fire events from calibrated dates

methods.description: This method calculates various metrics for charcoal and phytoliths to analyze fire and vegetation history.

steps.description: Calculate the total charcoal abundance per core and per depth interval.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate total charcoal abundance per core and depth interval

steps.description: Calculate the proportion of depth intervals containing charcoal for each core.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate proportion of depth intervals with charcoal
outputs: proportion of depth intervals with charcoal

steps.description: Calculate the relative percentage of all phytolith types found within each sample.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate relative percentage of phytolith types per sample

steps.description: Perform detrended correspondence analysis to look for patterns of vegetation (dis)similarity.
steps.searchable_summary: perform detrended correspondence analysis for vegetation patterns

steps.description: Calculate trends and magnitude of change of palm and arboreal phytolith types within each core.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate trends and magnitude of change for phytolith types

steps.description: Determine association between phytolith and charcoal metrics using Spearman rank correlation tests.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Spearman rank correlation between phytolith and charcoal metrics

methods.description: This method describes the laboratory process for analyzing charcoal in soil samples, including boiling, sieving, and calculating surface area and volume.

steps.description: Boil 20-50 mg of soil samples in 3% H2O2 for 15 minutes.
steps.searchable_summary: boil soil samples in hydrogen peroxide

steps.description: Rinse and sieve the remaining material at 500 μm.
steps.searchable_summary: rinse and sieve soil material at 500 μm

steps.description: Identify charcoal fragments using a microscope and photograph them.
steps.searchable_summary: identify and photograph charcoal fragments using a microscope

steps.description: Calculate the surface area of each charcoal fragment using Image J software.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate charcoal fragment surface area with Image J

steps.description: Convert surface area to volume using Weng's equation.
steps.searchable_summary: convert charcoal surface area to volume using Weng's equation

steps.description: Derive the volume of charcoal per cubic centimeter of soil.
steps.searchable_summary: derive charcoal volume per cubic centimeter of soil

methods.description: This method outlines the preparation and analysis of phytoliths from soil samples, including chemical treatments and identification.

steps.description: Add 56,000 microspheres to each 1 cm³ soil sample at the onset of processing.
steps.searchable_summary: add microspheres to soil samples for concentration calculation

steps.description: Use H2O2, HCl, and KMnO4 to remove carbonates, organic material, and humic acids.
steps.searchable_summary: remove impurities from soil samples using chemical treatments

steps.description: Add Bromoform to separate phytoliths and microspheres from remaining soil material.
steps.searchable_summary: separate phytoliths using Bromoform

steps.description: Mount phytoliths on microscope slides using Naphrax.
steps.searchable_summary: mount phytoliths on slides with Naphrax

steps.description: Identify phytoliths using a microscope with 1,000× magnification and differential interference contrast.
steps.searchable_summary: identify phytoliths using high magnification microscope

steps.description: Count phytoliths until 200 arboreal and 300 total phytoliths are reached.
steps.searchable_summary: count phytoliths to specified totals
